body_txt: For a criminal, whether organized or otherwise, the main goal once they have succeeded in working and scheming their way to the top is to leave a legacy, and the most common way is to have a successor who will inherit it all from them and carry on their work. As criminal organizations are often a family affair, it thus makes perfect sense for this successor to be their direct offspring (or at least a member of their family), with the assumption that the next generation shall carry on their work and status. However, as is the problem in all organizations that rely on hereditary inheritance, there is always the danger that the next generation will fail to be able to live up to their example. Thus, despite their best efforts (or sometimes directly because of them ), our successful criminal is left with an utter failure for a son (or other younger relative). Despite the name, incompetent daughters are likewise possible, however, as most organised crime falls into an Heir Club for Men they are overall rarer. Grandsons, younger siblings or cousins and nephews can also qualify in the absence of on-screen sons. Generally, this plays out in five specific variations (or some combination):  Whatever the version, it will often involve said successful criminal trying to figure out what to do about their incompetent offspring. Perhaps they will be in denial about their failures out of love or pride, refusing to listen to anything to the contrary until it seriously blows up in their face. Or perhaps they will pull out all the stops in an attempt to force them to learn how to be a successful criminal (which will often fail if not completely backfire on them). If they do come to accept that they just don't have it in them and will be the destruction of everything they have spent their life building, do not be surprised if they make efforts to move them out of the lifestyle or even permanently force them out , often to nobody's chagrin . See also The Wrongful Heir to the Throne , Inadequate Inheritor , Sucksessor , Nepotism . Compare Overlord Jr. for supervillain parents. Contrast Daddy's Little Villain and Mafia Princess , who are usually more competent but kept away from power due to patriarchal traditions of organised crime.
